chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P. No. D – 1742 of 2019 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of case 1. For orders on office objection at Flag-A 2. For hearing of main case 18-11-2021 Mr. Muhammad Qayyum Arain, Advocate for the Petitioner..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- Through this Petition, the Petitioner seeks appointment with the Respondents. Learned Counsel submits that Petitioner applied for a job before the Respondents, and on 26-12-2017, a letter was issued for written test and interview; however, when the Petitioner approached their office, no interview or test was conducted. We have confronted the Petitioner’s Counsel as to what right has accrued on the basis of this letter to file instant Petition, and to this, he has not been able to satisfactorily respond. Even otherwise, Petition has been filed much after 2017, and therefore, even laches is also applied. Lastly, in Para-6 of the plaint, it is stated that Petitioner’s elders had donated a plot to the Respondents, and on that basis also, he is entitled for appointment. As to this, the Hon’ble Supreme Court in Civil Appeals No. 19-K to 50-K of 2015 (Re: Government of Sindh and others v. Loung Khan Rajper and others) vide order dated 09-11-2015 has already held that no appointment can be given on plot donation. In view of hereinabove facts and circumstances, no case for indulgence is made out, and therefore, the Petition is dismissed in limine. J U D G E J U D G E Abdul Basit
